steps.title: Determine the smaller map's side length
steps.content:
The smaller map is 70% shorter than the original, meaning it retains 30% of the original size.
$$\text{New side} = 45 \times 0.30 = 13.5 \text{ inches}$$

steps.title: Find the new scale using the constant geography principle
steps.content:
Both maps represent the same geographic region and must account for the same total distance.

Original map total: $45 \text{ inches} \times 13 \text{ miles/inch} = 585 \text{ miles}$

Smaller map: $13.5 \text{ inches} \times (\text{new scale}) = 585 \text{ miles}$
$$\text{New scale} = \frac{585}{13.5} = \frac{13 \times 45}{13.5} = 13 \times \frac{45}{13.5} = 13 \times \frac{10}{3} = \frac{130}{3} = 43.33 \text{ miles per inch}$$
This matches choice D — D is correct.
